caption:
🗼Can I complete 50 Tokyo Bucket List items in 5 days!?

Tokyo has SO much to offer that it can get super overwhelming- even as someone who’s lived here 3.5 years! So I’m challenging myself to explore more and got help from TOMOGO! and Yuko organised everything for Day 1 😊

These are the things on my bucket list and where Yuko ended up taking me 🏃🏻‍♂️

1. yummy bakery: le Bage
2. unique workshop experience: FUTO
3. traditional Japan: Kuramae Shrine
4. local hidden gem restaurant: Tempura Iseya
5. Japanese stationery: mt lab
6. pottery & home wares: proto
7. *viral* cafe: Dandelion Chocolate Cafe

all 7 spots were brand new to me and I genuinely had such an amazing time!! I truly recommend TOMOGO! for their expertise and guidance to make a day in Tokyo so jam-packed full of new experiences, knowledge and fun 🤭 I always get along with my guides and Yuko was exactly like hanging out with a long time friend~

Book your own personalised tour with TOMOGO! They are dedicated to highlighting lesser-known areas, local communities, and businesses 💙

🔗 link in bio or here:

https://www.tomogo-travel.com/exclusive-tours/omakase-tokyo

💸 SAVE 10% off with promo code EBII10 💸

#PR #traveljapan #japantrip #tokyojapan
transcript: japan only has five weekends left before sakura season but i still have 50 things i want to do before the travelers take over tokyo thankfully yuko from tomogo organized everything for day one we started off at le bag for breakfast because real bagels are rare here then yuko booked us an appointment at futo where we wrote letters to our future selves which i loved and i think it makes such a perfect souvenir because it doesn't add any luggage weight since they send it to you one year later then we stopped by kuramaya shrine for some good luck which was close by then for lunch we went to tempura iseya genuinely the best anago tempura of my life so surprised it's not viral yet but definitely worth the wait if there's a line i love japanese stationery so we went to this famous washi tape store and i bought these two for journaling i would have been way too scared to come up here all alone and it was giving old classroom vibes but i love seeing these japanese ceramics at proto we ended the day by skipping the line at dandelion chocolate cafe tried their famous hot chocolate and i ended up discovering my new favorite cookie in tokyo i ticked off seven things with 43 left all thanks to yuko for the personalized tomogo tour
